title: eriogonum nummulare
intro: Eriogonum nummulare, also known as desert trumpet or desert buckwheat, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its showy pink flowers and woolly foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce pain, and fight infections. The health benefits of Eriogonum nummulare include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. Eriogonum nummulare can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
